checks.detail: Instruction mentions correctness and speed but does not disclose that performance is 50% of the score or that the speed gate compares against cmark with a 5x factor.
reasons: Performance weight (50%) and cmark-relative 5x speed ratio are not inferable from instruction.md

checks.detail: CommonMark spec is linked and tricky terms (loose/tight, lazy continuation, list-item indentation) are defined in instruction.md.
Dockerfile installs gcc/make/build-essential and workspace ships Makefile + md2html.c stub matching the documented build commands.
solution/solve.sh exists (2359 bytes) and installs a token-gated cmark wrapper as the reference oracle.
Hidden cases cover the same three CommonMark sections (Lists, List items, Block quotes) declared in instruction.md, as structural variants.

notes: Consider disclosing in instruction.md that scoring weighs correctness and performance equally, and that performance is measured relative to a reference C CommonMark implementation — this helps the agent prioritize iteration time appropriately.
